The tests are failing because the `BayesApiError` is being mocked (the whole `@/lib/api/client` module is mocked), so `instanceof BayesApiError` returns false since the mocked `BayesApiError` is not the real constructor. The test creates a `new BayesApiError(404, "no_orders_found")` but because `BayesApiError` is mocked via `vi.mock`, the real constructor is lost.

I need to fix this. The issue is that the tests mock the entire `@/lib/api/client` module, which replaces `BayesApiError` with a mock. 

When the components check `error instanceof BayesApiError`, it fails because the mock version is different from what the test instantiates. I need to look at how existing tests like `Portfolio.test.tsx` handle this, since they're importing `BayesApiError` from the mocked module but the auto-mocking replaces the class with a mock object.

The real problem is that `vi.mock("@/lib/api/client")` auto-mocks the entire module, so `BayesApiError` becomes a mock constructor that doesn't properly set the error properties. When the test creates `new BayesApiError(404, "no_orders_found")`, it's creating an instance of the mock rather than the real class, which is why the `instanceof` check fails and the error falls through to the generic handler showing "Failed to load P&L".

I need to either manually implement the mock to preserve the real `BayesApiError` behavior or use a different approach to set up the error properly in the test.

Setting up a partial mock... I'm realizing the best approach is to keep BayesApiError real in the mock while only mocking the API functions, so I can use instanceof checks in the component without issues. I need to update both test files to use this pattern consistently.
